author: phase-4-coordinator
content:
Resolved. Added NumPy-style out= support via _into suffix functions for the most-used ops:

Binary: add_into, subtract_into, multiply_into, divide_into
Unary (memory-bound): sqrt_into, square_into, absolute_into, negative_into
Unary (compute-bound, parallel at 100k): exp_into, log_into, sin_into, cos_into

All are backed by three new helpers in ferray-ufunc/src/helpers.rs: unary_float_op_into, unary_float_op_into_compute, binary_float_op_into. These require contiguous inputs/output and identical shapes (broadcasting is not supported on the fast path — callers needing it should use the allocating variants, which is called out in the error message). The caller owns the output buffer so there's literally zero allocation per call.

15 new unit tests cover each variant's correctness plus shape-mismatch error handling, matching the non-into variant results, and a chainable-no-alloc loop pattern that runs 100 iterations over the same buffer.

Benchmark (release, cargo run --release --example bench_into):
  add n=100:     2.45x speedup (44ns -> 18ns)  — small-array alloc overhead
  add n=1M:      1.88x speedup (829us -> 441us) — large-Vec alloc cost
  add n=100k:    1.29x speedup
  exp n=100k:    1.23x speedup

218 lib + 50/19/53 integration tests pass. Clippy clean. Full workspace builds. Also fixed a pre-existing 'extra unused type parameters' clippy lint in the new check_into_shapes helper while here.
